texto: Peasants are people without sense or forethought. Therefore, they must not give rice to their wives and children at harvest time, but must save food for the future. They should eat millet, vegetables, and other coarse food instead of rice. Even the fallen leaves of plants should be saved as food against famine.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Keian no Ofuregaki sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Peasants are people without sense or forethought. Therefore, they must not give rice to their wiv…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, food, governance, útil em momentos de governance e japan.
